California
Fair POlitical
Practices Commission
March 2■
′ ■99o
Larry lsrae■
■625 01ympic B■vd.
Santa Monica′  cA 90404
Dear Mr. Israel:
Re:  Your Request For Adv■
ce
Our File No. A-90-■
25
You have requested advice concerning the campaign disclosure
provisions of the PoliticaI Reform Act.1/
OUESTION
What nethod should be used to determine the value of air
transportation provided to a candidate for purposes of receiving
adequate payment from the candidate?
CONCLUSION
The candidate must pay the fair market value of the air
transportation, which can be determined by using the commercial
air rate or the charter rate divided by the number of -passengers.
If less than the fair market value is paid for the service, a non-
monetary contribution has been made to the candidate.
FACTS
-You are a pilot and own an airplane. you may provide a
candidate for state office with air transportation Lo various
speaking engagements.
Ll 
covernment code sections Blooo-91o15. ArI statutory
references are to the Government code unless otherwise indicated.
Comrnission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18OOO, et seq. AII references to regulations ari to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Strcct,Suitc 800 0 P.o.BO、807 0 Sacran、cnto(〕A95804‐0807 0 (916)322‐ 5660


ANALYSIS
The term rr contributiontt includes:
...a payment except to the extent that fulL and adequate
consideration is received unless it is clear fron the
surrounding circurnstances that it is not made for political
purposes. (section 82015. )
rrFull and adequate considerationri as used in Section 82015
neans the rrfair market valuerrr or lrhatever it rrouLd cost the
candidate to obtain the same or similar services on the open
market. (Section 82025.5.) In In re Stone (L977) 3 FppC Ops. 52
at 58 (copy enclosed), the Connission stated that the fair market
val.ue of private air transportation can be determined by using
either the commercial air rate or the charter rate divided by the
nunber of passengers.
If a candidate pays Less than the fair narket value for the
air transportation you provide, you have made a non-rnonetary
contribution to the candidate -
The Act places linitations 
on contributions to candidates for
elective office based on a fiscal year (July 1 - June 30).
(Sections 853o1-85303.) Monetary and non-monetary contributions
as well as loans and enforceable promises from a irpersonr. to a
candidate are limited to 91,ooo per fiscal year. lsection s53o1.)
A.'rpersonrr includes an individual, corporation, proprietorship,
firrn, partnership or cotnpany. (Section 85102(b).)
If you have any questions regarding this letter, please
contact ne at (916) 322-5662.
t "rry tsrael
Page Two
S incerely,
Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel
AZ.-fu./"-

Analysis Division

CONCLUSION: The candidate must pay the fair market value of the air
